nameMeanings.name: Llywelyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Llewellyn
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Llywelyn, derived from British Celtic Lugubelinos, itself from the divine name Lugus + an uncertain second element often used alone as a name. Through association with llew ‘lion’, it was altered to Llewelyn. This was the name of two princes of north Wales who held the Normans at bay for many years in the 13th century. Etymologically speaking, the form with a fourth -l- is an error, not found as an original Welsh name, but this is now much the most common spelling in the US.

nameMeanings.name: Ieuaf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

nameMeanings.name: Goch
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

en.lifeSummary: When Ieuaf Goch Llywelyn was born about 1290, in Caernarfonshire, Wales, United Kingdom, his father, Llywelyn ap Maredudd Bengoch, was 46 and his mother, Mrs. Llywelyn ap Maredudd Bengoch, was 42. He had at least 8 sons and 1 daughter with Mrs. Ieuaf Goch Llywelyn.
